mechanism_narrative: Persistently low absolute levels of the federal funds rate keep the equity risk premium wide relative to the risk-free rate, sustaining a positive drift in equity valuations. As long as FEDFUNDS remains below its trailing 5-year average, forward 20-day S&P 500 returns should be positively biased through continued discount-rate support.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.41 percent as of Nov 2016, near multi-decade lows
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or mean forward return in low-rate periods is not significantly greater than in high-rate periods (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sudden monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals an abrupt deterioration in labor demand that historically precedes risk-off repricing in equities within days as markets reassess growth and earnings expectations. A rise of more than 0.2 percentage points in UNRATE month-over-month triggers negative forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6 percent as of Nov 2016
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across all historical trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly capture consumer and residential investment demand; when both accelerate simultaneously it signals broad-based demand strength that supports sustained equity gains over a full quarter, whereas divergence between the two dampens the signal. Neither series alone reliably predicts returns, but their joint positive momentum does.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST 3-month rate of change positive simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 465513 million and HOUST at 1323 thousand units as of late 2016
failure_condition: Joint-positive-momentum periods do not show statistically higher 60-day forward returns than periods where only one series is positive (t-test p>0.05), or interaction term adds no explanatory power over additive single-series model.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, decelerating industrial production growth signals approaching capacity constraints and margin compression that markets price in advance, producing negative forward equity returns over the next month. This mechanism is specific to late-cycle regimes and does not hold in early recovery phases where INDPRO deceleration reflects noise rather than structural slowdown.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 6-month annualized growth decelerates below 1 percent
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.8589 as of Nov 2016, index 2017=100
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating industrial output
failure_condition: In late-cycle windows, forward 20-day returns following INDPRO deceleration are not significantly negative (p>0.05), or the effect reverses sign when tested against an independently dated business-cycle classification.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects durable labor market expansion that supports corporate revenue and consumer spending over a full quarter horizon, generating a persistent positive drift in equity prices as long as payroll growth trend remains intact.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 145128 thousand persons as of Nov 2016
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 6-month PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected upward surprise in the federal funds rate, such as a 25bp hike at an FOMC meeting after a prolonged pause, triggers a short-term repricing of discount rates and risk assets, producing negative equity returns in the immediate days following the move as markets digest tighter policy stance.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.20pp within a single month following at least 6 months of no change
evidence_refs: fact:FOMC hiked target range to 0.50-0.75 percent on Dec 14 2016 after a full year without a hike
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following hike events are positive in more than 55 percent of historical instances, or the effect is not distinguishable from random noise at p>0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising realized inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a rising federal funds rate jointly compress real yields' cushion and signal an active tightening cycle; the interaction of accelerating CPI and increasing FEDFUNDS produces stronger negative pressure on equity valuations than either series alone, because markets price forward tightening expectations more aggressively when both inflation and policy rate rise together.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth rising and FEDFUNDS rising simultaneously over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 242.348 and FEDFUNDS at 0.41 as of Nov 2016 amid a newly initiated hiking cycle
failure_condition: Joint CPI-and-rate-rising periods do not produce significantly more negative 20-day forward returns than periods with only one series rising (p>0.05), or interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 2.5 percent annualized signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings expansion over the following quarter, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon following the GDP release that confirms above-trend growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth exceeds 2.5 percent
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16712.5 billion chained 2017 dollars as of mid-2016 estimate
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns following above-2.5-percent GDP prints are not significantly greater than the unconditional baseline mean (t-test p>0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
